title: coursetia ferruginea
intro: Coursetia ferruginea, also known as the horsewood or horse-wood tree, is a plant species native to southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Coursetia ferruginea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the specific preparation and dosage.
